title: Therapy as Re-Reading
description:
‘What if a new interpretation endangers the self that produced it?’

Claudia Grigg Edo on therapy as re-reading.

The post Therapy as Re-Reading appeared first on Granta.
hook: Your therapist isn't interpreting you—they're rewriting you. What happens when understanding yourself threatens who you are?

title: Hacks was always a love story
description: The beauty of HBO Max's smash hit comedy lies in the relationship between Deborah and Ava that developed over five seasons.
hook: Five seasons of a comedy about comedians that's really about two women learning to need each other. The love story nobody expected.

title: Diaspora, Indebted: On “Industry”
description:
“Gambling with big bets against challenging odds, regarded warily by moneylenders, questioned about your ability to balance your books, needing to take on more debt to pay back older debt. Whom exactly are we talking about here: Rishi or Britain?”

The post Diaspora, Indebted: On “Industry” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: A British prestige drama about finance workers becomes a savage metaphor for post-colonial debt and who actually owes whom.

title: Tending the Fire: Exploring AIDS Writing of the Last Ten Years
description: There are countless testimonies, books, poetry collections, plays, visual works and films from queer people amid the AIDS crisis of the 1980s and 90s, surviving in the midst of so much death. Many of these works have far outlived their
hook: A decade of AIDS writing that survived when its writers didn't—poetry, plays, testimony that outlasted the crisis itself.

title: Meet Me in the Darkroom
description: The booming Bushwick club scene has lately drawn the attention of “serious” writers and thinkers. Staff writers at prestigious magazines and tenured faculty at elite schools are openly talking about going to illegal warehouse raves and taking illegal drugs—but doing so deeply, politically, radically.
hook: Serious writers are showing up at Bushwick nightclubs. What are they really looking for in the dark?
